item.title: Michele Bullock: Opening Statement to the Senate Economics Legislation Committee (Budget Estimates 2026–2027)
item.speaker: Michele Bullock

rating.summary: RBA Governor Michele Bullock addressed the Senate Economics Legislation Committee, outlining the Bank's decision to raise the cash rate by 75 basis points in 2026 in response to a resurgence in inflation driven by strong domestic demand and a Middle East conflict boosting energy prices. She indicated inflation remains too high, with headline CPI expected to peak above 4.5% in the June quarter and underlying inflation staying above target until mid-2027. The Board signalled it will do whatever is necessary to restore price stability, even as growth and employment are expected to soften.
rating.stance_rationale: The speech is clearly hawkish: the RBA has already delivered 75bp in rate hikes this year, inflation forecasts are elevated and rising, and Bullock explicitly warned of second-round effects and the risk of embedded inflation, while pledging to do 'what it considers necessary' to achieve price stability. No easing bias is expressed.
key_quotes: Inflation is too high, and the Board will do what it considers necessary to achieve our mandate to deliver price stability and full employment.

If high inflation persists, it risks becoming embedded in price and wage-setting behaviour...That would result in more persistent inflation and would require even higher interest rates, and for longer, to return inflation to target.
